params.param: Path of the working directory in the host.
Path of the working directory.
Name of the input FASTA file containing the sequences to be annotated (relative to the working directory)
Name of the FASTA file containing the reference sequence (relative to the working directory)

Maximum distance between exons (in this case sequences identified by getorf) from the same gene. It only applies to large genome sequences where there is some chance that two genes with similar features are present.
Distance around the junction point between two sequences where to look for splicing signals.

The selection model to be used (1, 2, or 3): 1) similarity with reference sequence first, in case of a tie, percentage of gaps relative to reference sequence; 2) percentage of gaps relative to reference sequence first, in case of a tie, similarity with reference sequence; 3) a mixed model with similarity with reference sequence first, but if fewer gaps relative to reference sequence, similarity gets a bonus defined by the user. Currently, a bonus of 20, means 2%.
A bonus percentage times 10. For instance, 20 means 2% bonus. Something with 18% similarity acts as having 20% similarity. Applied when selection_criterion=3.
Minimum size for CDS to be reported.
params.flag: Use this flag to tell CGA that the input FASTA is a genome and extract genome regions first.
params.param: The maximum sequence lenght allowed in input FASTA files when the input_fasta_is_genome is not set.
When the input is a genome, the maximum distance between BLAST hits to be considered the same region.
When the input is a genome, the number of nucleotides to be included 5' and 3' of the region retrieved in the BLAST search.
When the input is a genome, the expectation value for tblastn.
params.flag: Use this flag to skip the pull-docker-images task.
params.param: Version of the pegi3s/seqkit image to use.
Version of the pegi3s/blast image to use.
Version of the pegi3s/emboss image to use.
Version of the pegi3s/utilities image to use.
Version of the pegi3s/blast_utilities image to use.
Version of the pegi3s/cds2gff3-maker image to use.
Path of the directory containing the pipeline scripts.
params.flag: Use this flag to keep the temporary, intermediate files generated by the pipeline tasks (useful for debugging).

metadata.task-description: Downloads the neccessary Docker images for external software (BLAST, seqkit, etc.).

Initializes the internal working directory. Also, if the input_fasta_is_genome flag is disabled, checks if all sequences in the input FASTA have a length lower than the specified maximum_not_genome_length.
When the input_fasta_is_genome flag is used, extracts the genome regions to be analized from the input genome FASTA first.
Splits the input FASTA file into several FASTA files containing one input sequence each.
Obtains all between STOP codons open reading frames (ORF) longer than 30 bp using EMBOSS getorf.
Creates a protein BLAST database for each file from the previous step and performs a blastp using the reference FASTA file.
Sorts files to ensure that exons are ordered according to their relative location in the genome, using the information that the EMBOSS getorf program outputs in the sequence headers.
Runs the 6 sub-steps for joining exons iteratively until all exons have been successfully joined. Check the documentation for more details about these 6 sub-steps.

Processes the output of the previous step using a combination of the EMBOSS getorf and transeq programs to obtain the predicted CDS and protein sequences (only those in frame +1 are considered) only if they are longer than the minimum size specified by the user in the min_full_nucleotide_size parameter.
Aggregates the results from each input sequence analysed to generate the aggregated output files under the results directory.
